methodology: CFPB HMDA aggregate denial reason codes (2018-2025), weighted by year, normalized to 100%. Reason codes: DTI=debt-to-income ratio, Credit History=credit score/history, Collateral=property/LTV, Incomplete=documentation gaps, Other=remaining.
caveat: Reason-level HMDA aggregates; individual lender exact percentages may vary. Patterns directionally validated against CFPB public snapshots.

CrossCountry.profile: DTI-focused screener — willing to work with credit history if DTI clean
CrossCountry.note: Low overall denial rate (6.37%) means they see fewer edge cases

Guild.note: Manual underwriting appetite shows in incomplete app tolerance

Rocket.note: Digital-first = low incomplete rate, high credit-history denial

UWM.profile: Broker channel = more incomplete apps at submission
UWM.note: High incomplete rate reflects broker pipeline quality variance

Wells Fargo.profile: Traditional bank: credit score and collateral first, DTI secondary
Wells Fargo.note: Collateral weight reflects remaining conventional bank mentality

NewRez.profile: Regime-sensitive: tightens across all categories when OFI > 50
NewRez.note: In high-OFI environments, incomplete + collateral reasons spike

Freedom.profile: Volume-driven pipeline: incomplete applications are the #1 killer
Freedom.note: OFI-sensitive: when markets tighten, incomplete rate rises further

PennyMac.profile: Improving trend — reason mix becoming more like CrossCountry/Guild
PennyMac.note: Historical genome overstates current strictness (use 2024-25 data)

Mr. Cooper.profile: Servicing-first lender — credit profile consistency matters

loanDepot.profile: Pipeline-driven — retail channel shows in reason distribution

Planet Home.profile: Specialty channel — incomplete apps reflect complex file types
